title: Strings 3
description: Strings can be concatenated using the `+` operator or the `format!` macro.
instructions: Fix the string concatenation to make the code compile.
hints: Use `.trim()` to remove whitespace from both ends of a string.
Use `format!` macro to concatenate strings, or use `+` operator.
explanation: Rust strings have many built-in methods. `.trim()` removes whitespace from both ends (returning a `&str`). The `format!` macro works like `println!` but returns a `String` instead of printing. `.replace()` creates a new `String` with all occurrences of a pattern replaced.
